+++ dst://docs/.feature/VAL-26642/stories/story-A-merge-and-final-product.md
@@ -0,0 +1,114 @@
+# Story A — Merge step + Final Product Details (VAL-26642)
+
+> Groups slices **S1 + S2 + S3 + S6**. Self-contained, AI-implementable. Mirrors the already-migrated
+> Upgrade Process integrate-changes stage. **Migrate to the new domains architecture only — never depend on legacy.**
+
+## Goal
+Migrate the CI / Build & Test **merge step** (a.k.a. "Integrate Changes") from the legacy MFE
+(`web/apps/ci-process-mfe`) into the **domains/business-process** stack, plus add the new **Final Product Details**
+section. Visual + behavioural parity with the legacy merge step; new UI per Figma.
+
+## Scope (what this story delivers)
+- A new CI execution view + integrate-changes stage under
+  `web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/build-and-test-process` (NEW).
+- Merge tab composed from existing shared widgets (parity with Upgrade).
+- A **new Final Product Details widget** in `domains/artifact/widget` (+ new service in `domains/artifact/data-access`).
+- Decision / Stopped states + info alerts (S6).
+- All required **data-access services + models migrated to domains, with unit tests AND contract (pact) tests.**
+
+## Out of scope
+- Backports tabs (v1 legacy + v2 ag-grid) → **Story B**.
+
+## Figma & visual references
+| Ref | What it shows |
+|-----|---------------|
+| [9769-56784](https://www.figma.com/design/8Z7emdDFkZapK3nmVP2HsA/MxEvolve?node-id=9769-56784&m=dev) | Merge step — under-review state |
+| [9769-57183](https://www.figma.com/design/8Z7emdDFkZapK3nmVP2HsA/MxEvolve?node-id=9769-57183&m=dev) | Merge step — review variant |
+| [10333-56610](https://www.figma.com/design/8Z7emdDFkZapK3nmVP2HsA/MxEvolve?node-id=10333-56610&m=dev) | Merge step — merged state |
+| [9969-144576](https://www.figma.com/design/8Z7emdDFkZapK3nmVP2HsA/MxEvolve?node-id=9969-144576&m=dev) | New Final Product Details design |
+| `devo/.feature/VAL-26642/wiki-context/img1.png` | FP failure state |
+
+## Architecture conventions (MUST follow — upgrade-process template)
+- Standalone components; `input.required<T>()` / `input<T>()`; `rxResource` for fetch; `computed()`/`signal()`/`effect()`.
+- `host: { style: "display: contents;" }`; no NgRx; no `ngOnInit` fetch.
+- Layout via `mxevolve-stage-container` + `mxevolve-business-process-content-container` (header/footer slots).
+- Lazy `loadComponent` route added to `web/apps/shell/.../business-process-routing.module.ts`.
+- Cross-domain imports via barrels only: `@mxevolve/domains/business-process/{feature,composite-widget,ui,data-access,util}`,
+  `@mxevolve/domains/scm/widget`, `@mxevolve/domains/artifact/{widget,data-access}`.
+- **Nx boundary:** a domains lib MUST NOT depend on `web/libs/features/*` (tagged `type:legacy`).
+
+## Verified component signatures to reuse (from real source files)
+| Component (barrel) | Selector | Inputs |
+|--------------------|----------|--------|
+| `IntegrateChangesStageComponent` (upgrade-process) — structural template | — | `projectId`, `processId`, `latestMergeRequestId`, `developmentId`, `stageStatus`, `supportsResourceManagement`, `parentBranchName` |
+| scm/widget | `mxevolve-merge-request-stepper` | `mergeRequestId`, `projectId` |
+| composite-widget | `mxevolve-retry-merge-request` | `projectId`, `processId`, `developmentId`, `supportsResourceManagement`, `parentBranchName`, `stageStatus` |
+| composite-widget | `mxevolve-fix-issues` | `projectId`, `processId`, `stageStatus` |
+| feature (template ref) | `UpgradeProcessExecutionViewComponent` | `projectId`, `executionId` — uses `ExecutionFetcherService`, `rxResource`, `steps` computed, `selectedStepId` synced to `?step=` query param |
+
+## Data-access migration (REQUIRED — new architecture, no legacy reliance)
+Every service + model this story needs must be **migrated into the new domains data-access libs**, each with its
+**unit tests AND contract (pact) tests**. Copy from the legacy CI MFE / `features/*` equivalents, then **enhance** to
+match the new code (signal/`inject()` style, new model shapes, new barrels). **Do NOT import or depend on legacy libs.**
+
+**In `domains/business-process/data-access`:**
+- **CI execution fetcher service + models** — returns the Build & Test execution including: `ciVersion`,
+  `backportRequested`, `willPublishFinalProduct`, `finalProductId`, `backportFinalProductId`,
+  `integrateDestinationBranch`, the merge stage + `latestMergeRequestId`, `developmentId`,
+  `supportsResourceManagement`, `parentBranchName`, and stage statuses.
+  - Source to copy/enhance: legacy `ci-process-execution` state/services in `web/apps/ci-process-mfe`.
+- **Merge / user-input action services** (send-changes-for-review, reopen-merge-request, fix-integration-issues) —
+  these already exist in `domains/business-process/data-access` (Upgrade uses them). **Reuse** them; only add a
+  CI-specific payload/model if the request body differs. Endpoints unchanged.
+
+**In `domains/artifact/data-access` (only `factory-product` exists today — final-product is NEW):**
+- **FinalProductService + models** — fetch a final product by `finalProductId`; expose in-progress / success / failure
+  states needed by the FP widget.
+  - Source to copy/enhance: legacy final-product service in `web/libs/features/artifact-manager`.
+
+**Contract (pact) tests — convention:**
+- New domains data-access libs currently have **no** pact tests. Add them to the **shared
+  `web/libs/contract-tests/src/lib/`** lib (pattern: `scm-service.spec.pact.ts`), importing the **new** domains
+  services via their barrels. Providers:
+  - `business-process-execution-service` (CI execution fetch + user-input actions).
+  - `artifact-management-service` (final product fetch).
+- Reuse matchers from the legacy CI pact
+  `web/apps/ci-process-mfe/src/contracts/business-process-execution-service.spec.pact.ts` and **enhance** for the new
+  model fields.
+- Verify locally with the **local-pact-verify** skill before merge.
+
+## Tasks (ordered)
+1. **Data-access first.** Create the CI execution fetcher service + models in `domains/business-process/data-access`
+   (copy/enhance from legacy CI MFE). Add unit tests + pact test in `web/libs/contract-tests`.
+2. Create `FinalProductService` + models in `domains/artifact/data-access` (copy/enhance from legacy artifact-manager).
+   Add unit tests + pact test in `web/libs/contract-tests`.
+3. **S1 — Scaffold:** Create `build-and-test-process` feature lib; CI execution view (`projectId`, `executionId`)
+   using `rxResource` + the new fetcher; `steps` computed; `selectedStepId` synced to `?step=`; integrate-changes
+   stage shell. Wire lazy route in the shell. Expose `ciVersion`/`backportRequested`/`willPublishFinalProduct`/
+   `finalProductId` to the stage.
+4. **S2 — Merge tab:** Compose `mxevolve-merge-request-stepper` + branch-details + `mxevolve-retry-merge-request` +
+   `mxevolve-fix-issues` + send-for-review/reopen. Replicate state-based button gating (`areActionsDisabled`,
+   `ciProcessStatus === FAILED`). Match Figma 9769-56784 / 9769-57183 / 10333-56610.
+5. **S3 — Final Product Details:** Build `mxevolve-final-product-details` in `domains/artifact/widget` (`type:widget`,
+   `scope:artifact`); render when `willPublishFinalProduct` && `finalProductId`; in-progress shows `-` placeholders;
+   success + failure (banner) states. Wire optional `finalProductId` into the stage. Match Figma 9969-144576 + img1.
+6. **S6 — Decision/Stopped + alerts:** integrate-changes-decision (Stopped + requester), info alerts wording.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+- CI merge step loads via the shell route in `domains/business-process`, mirroring upgrade-process structure.
+- Merge tab is visually + behaviourally at parity with the Upgrade merge step; all actions work; gating is state-based.
+- Final Product Details renders **only** when `willPublishFinalProduct` && `finalProductId`; covers in-progress (`-`),
+  success, and failure states; lives in `domains/artifact`; **no** dependency on `type:legacy` artifact-manager.
+- No feature flags / authorization added to the merge step (parity — it had none).
+- **All consumed data-access is migrated to domains** with unit tests + passing contract (pact) tests; nothing imports legacy.
+
+## Tests
+- Unit: execution view renders the correct stage by status; fetcher maps CI fields; FP widget state branches; merge
+  button enable/disable per state; user-input actions call correct services.
+- Contract: pact specs for `business-process-execution-service` (execution + actions) and `artifact-management-service`
+  (final product), verified via local-pact-verify.
+
+## Definition of Done
+- Lint + Nx module boundaries pass (no domains→legacy edges).
+- Unit + contract tests green.
+- Visual/behavioural parity confirmed against legacy merge step and the referenced Figma nodes.
diff --git src://docs/.feature/VAL-26642/stories/story-B-backports.md dst://docs/.feature/VAL-26642/stories/story-B-backports.md
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..d637340f51a
--- /dev/null
+++ dst://docs/.feature/VAL-26642/stories/story-B-backports.md
@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@
+# Story B — Backports tabs (v2 ag-grid + v1 legacy) (VAL-26642)
+
+> Groups slices **S4 + S5**. Depends on **Story A** (CI execution view + stage shell must exist first).
+> **Migrate to the new domains architecture only — never depend on legacy.**
+
+## Goal
+Migrate the **Backports** experience of the CI merge step. Two renderings driven by `ciVersion`:
+- **v2** — a "Backports" summary tab with two ag-grid tables + an error banner moved above them (new Figma).
+- **v1** — legacy per-backport tabs copied **as-is** into a `legacy/` subfolder, **de-remoted** (remote MFE loading removed).
+
+## Scope
+- v2 Backports summary tab (ag-grid) — S4.
+- v1 legacy per-backport tabs, de-remoted — S5.
+- Backport-related data-access services + models migrated to domains, with unit + contract tests.
+
+## Out of scope
+- Merge tab, Final Product Details, scaffold, decision/alerts → **Story A**.
+
+## Figma & visual references
+| Ref | What it shows |
+|-----|---------------|
+| [9769-57211](https://www.figma.com/design/8Z7emdDFkZapK3nmVP2HsA/MxEvolve?node-id=9769-57211&m=dev) | v2 On-Demand Backport Executions table |
+| `devo/.feature/VAL-26642/wiki-context/img3.png` | v2 backports design |
+| `devo/.feature/VAL-26642/wiki-context/9769-57211.png` | exported backports table |
+| `devo/.feature/VAL-26642/wiki-context/img2.png` | legacy cherry-pick `@switch` code (v1 reference) |
+
+## Architecture conventions (MUST follow)
+- Same domains/upgrade-process conventions as Story A (standalone, signals, barrels, `display: contents`, no NgRx).
+- v2 tables use the **shared ag-grid wrapper** (not a hand-rolled table).
+- **Nx boundary:** domains lib MUST NOT depend on `web/libs/features/*` (`type:legacy`).
+- **De-remoting:** the legacy backport MR view was loaded from scm-mfe via `RemoteComponentInjectorService`. The remote
+  MFE is being removed — copy the underlying view **as-is** into `legacy/` and render it **without** the remote injector.
+
+## Verified component signature (v1 reference)
+| Legacy component | Inputs | Notes |
+|------------------|--------|-------|
+| `backport-cherry-pick-and-merge-request.component` (ci-process-mfe) | `projectId`, `repositoryId`, `ciProcessExecutionId`, `backport` | `@switch` on `CherryPickStatus` (in-progress / manual / picked) |
+
+## Data-access migration (REQUIRED — new architecture, no legacy reliance)
+Every service + model this story needs must be **migrated into `domains/business-process/data-access`**, each with its
+**unit tests AND contract (pact) tests**. Copy from the legacy CI MFE equivalents, then **enhance** to match the new
+code. **Do NOT import or depend on legacy libs.**
+
+- **CI backport executions service + models** — list backport executions with status (for the v2 executions table) and
+  per-backport detail (for v1 tabs incl. `cherryPickStatus`, `backportFinalProductId`).
+  - Source: legacy `CiProcessExecutionsService` in `web/apps/ci-process-mfe`.
+- **Business-process definitions service + models** — failed-to-launch backport definitions by definition name (v2
+  failed-to-launch table) + deleted/missing-definition info for the banner.
+  - Source: legacy `BusinessProcessDefinitionService` in `web/apps/ci-process-mfe`.
+- **Backport user-input actions** (repush-backport-merge-job, commits-cherry-picked) — reuse the migrated
+  user-input services in `domains/business-process/data-access` from Story A; add CI-specific payloads only if they differ.
+
+**Contract (pact) tests — convention:**
+- Add pact specs to the shared **`web/libs/contract-tests/src/lib/`** lib (pattern: `scm-service.spec.pact.ts`),
+  importing the **new** domains services via barrels. Provider: `business-process-execution-service`.
+- Reuse matchers from `web/apps/ci-process-mfe/src/contracts/business-process-execution-service.spec.pact.ts`; enhance
+  for new model fields. Verify with the **local-pact-verify** skill before merge.
+
+## Tasks (ordered)
+1. **Data-access first.** Migrate the CI backport executions service + models and the BP definitions service + models
+   into `domains/business-process/data-access` (copy/enhance from legacy). Add unit tests + pact specs in
+   `web/libs/contract-tests`.
+2. **S4 — v2 Backports tab:** "Backports" tab with **two ag-grid tables** (backport executions w/ status; failed-to-launch
+   definitions by definition name) using the shared ag-grid wrapper. Move the deleted/missing-definition **error banner
+   ABOVE** the tables (with ids). Preserve the info alert (verbatim strings). Match Figma 9769-57211 / img3.
+3. **S5 — v1 legacy tabs:** Copy per-backport tabs into a `legacy/` subfolder, **de-remoted**: cherry-pick `@switch`
+   (in-progress / manual / picked), manual-cherry-pick, backport MR view (no remote injector), repush-backport action,
+   per-backport branch details + final product. Behaviour identical to legacy.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+- **v2:** both ag-grid tables render; executions table shows status; failed-to-launch table shows definition names;
+  deleted-definition banner appears **above** the tables with ids; info alert wording matches current code verbatim;
+  layout matches Figma 9769-57211 / img3.
+- **v1:** each `cherryPickStatus` branch renders the correct sub-component; repush gating preserved; cherry-pick-done
+  flow works; renders **without** the remote injector; copy-as-is under `legacy/`; behaviour identical to legacy MFE.
+- **All consumed data-access is migrated to domains** with unit tests + passing contract (pact) tests; nothing imports legacy.
+
+## Tests
+- Unit: both ag-grid tables render correctly; banner-above ordering; each `cherryPickStatus` branch; repush gating;
+  legacy view renders without remote injector; services map fields correctly.
+- Contract: pact specs for `business-process-execution-service` (backport executions + BP definitions), verified via
+  local-pact-verify.
+
+## Definition of Done
+- Lint + Nx boundaries pass (no domains→legacy edges; no remote injector usage).
+- Unit + contract tests green.
+- v2 matches Figma + same behaviour as current code; v1 behaviour identical to legacy.

+++ dst://docs/.feature/VAL-26642/story-map.md
@@ -0,0 +1,68 @@
+# Story Map — VAL-26642: CI / Build & Test Merge Step
+
+> Suggestions only. This does NOT create or modify Jira tickets.
+
+## Grouped Stories (execution units)
+The 6 slices below are grouped into **2 implementable stories**. Each story migrates all its consumed
+data-access into the new domains architecture **with unit tests + contract (pact) tests** — never depending on legacy.
+
+| Story | Slices | Summary | Doc |
+|-------|--------|---------|-----|
+| **Story A** | S1 + S2 + S3 + S6 | Scaffold + Merge tab + Final Product Details + decision/alerts | [stories/story-A-merge-and-final-product.md](stories/story-A-merge-and-final-product.md) |
+| **Story B** | S4 + S5 | Backports v2 (ag-grid) + v1 legacy (de-remoted) | [stories/story-B-backports.md](stories/story-B-backports.md) |
+
+```mermaid
+graph LR
+  A[Story A - merge + final product] --> B[Story B - backports]
+```
+
+> **Story B depends on Story A** (the CI execution view + integrate-changes stage shell from S1 must exist first).
+
+## Suggested Story Slices
+| # | Slice | Summary | Layer(s) | Suggested owner/team | Required reviewers |
+|---|-------|---------|----------|----------------------|--------------------|
+| S1 | Scaffold `build-and-test-process` feature lib + stage shell | Create CI execution view + integrate-changes stage shell under `domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/build-and-test-process`, wire route in shell, fetch CI execution (rxResource), expose `ciVersion`/`backportRequested`/`willPublishFinalProduct`/`finalProductId`. | FE | ATLANTIS web | BP/SCM owners |
+| S2 | Merge tab (parity with Upgrade) | Compose merge-request-stepper + branch-details + retry-merge-request + fix-issues + send-for-review/reopen; replicate state-based button gating (`areActionsDisabled`). | FE | ATLANTIS web | BP/SCM owners |
+| S3 | New Final Product Details widget (`domains/artifact`) | Build a new FP widget in `domains/artifact/widget` + final-product service in `domains/artifact/data-access` (NOT the legacy `features/artifact-manager` one); render when `willPublishFinalProduct` + `finalProductId`; in-progress (`-` placeholders) + success + failure states; wire optional `finalProductId` into the stage. | FE | ATLANTIS web | BP / artifact owners |
+| S4 | Backports — v2 summary tab (ag-grid) | "Backports" tab: keep the two tables (backport executions w/ status; failed-to-launch definitions by definition name) using **ag-grid** (shared wrapper); move deleted/missing-definition error **banner above** the tables; preserve info alert. | FE | ATLANTIS web | BP owners |
+| S5 | Backports — v1 legacy tabs (copy as-is, `legacy/`) | Copy per-backport tabs into a `legacy/` subfolder, **de-remoted**: cherry-pick `@switch` (in-progress/manual/picked), manual-cherry-pick, backport MR view (no remote injector), repush backport action, per-backport branch details + final product. | FE | ATLANTIS web | BP/SCM owners |
+| S6 | Decision/Stopped states + alerts | integrate-changes-decision (Stopped + requester), backport decision-maker, info alerts wording for v1/v2. | FE | ATLANTIS web | BP owners |
+
+## Dependency Order
+```mermaid
+graph LR
+  S1 --> S2
+  S2 --> S3
+  S2 --> S4
+  S2 --> S5
+  S2 --> S6
+```
+
+## Parallelization Opportunities
+- After **S2** lands, **S3 / S4 / S5 / S6** are largely independent and can proceed in parallel (different sub-sections of the stage).
+- **S5** (legacy copy-as-is) is the riskiest for parity but has no dependency on S3/S4.
+
+## Per-Slice Test Obligations & Definition of Done
+### S1 — Scaffold
+- **Tests:** unit test execution-view renders stage by status; fetcher/mapper maps CI fields; route resolves.
+- **DoD:** CI execution view loads via shell route; integrate-changes stage shell renders; lint + Nx boundaries pass.
+
+### S2 — Merge tab
+- **Tests:** stepper sub-steps render; buttons enabled/disabled per state; send-for-review/reopen/fix-issues call correct services.
+- **DoD:** Visual + behavioural parity with Upgrade merge step; all merge actions work.
+
+### S3 — Final Product
+- **Tests:** new `domains/artifact/widget` FP component shown only when `willPublishFinalProduct` + `finalProductId`; in-progress shows `-` placeholders; failure shows error banner; data-access service fetch covered.
+- **DoD:** Behaviour matches legacy final-product UI; lives in `domains/artifact`; no dependency on `type:legacy` artifact-manager.
+
+### S4 — Backports v2
+- **Tests:** both ag-grid tables render; executions table shows status; failed-to-launch table shows definition names; deleted-definition banner appears **above** the tables with ids; info alert wording.
+- **DoD:** Matches new Figma (9769-57211 / img3) + same behaviour as current code.
+
+### S5 — Backports v1
+- **Tests:** each `cherryPickStatus` branch renders correct sub-component; repush gating; cherry-pick-done flow; renders without remote injector.
+- **DoD:** Behaviour identical to legacy MFE; copy-as-is under `legacy/`, de-remoted, verified.
+
+### S6 — Decision/alerts
+- **Tests:** Stopped state shows requester; alert text matches v1/v2.
+- **DoD:** Parity with legacy.
diff --git src://docs/.feature/VAL-26642/test-strategy.md dst://docs/.feature/VAL-26642/test-strategy.md
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..f589e41d6c5
--- /dev/null
+++ dst://docs/.feature/VAL-26642/test-strategy.md
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+# Test Strategy — VAL-26642: CI Merge Step
+
+## Risk-Based Focus
+| Area | Risk | Test type(s) | Priority |
+|------|------|--------------|----------|
+| Legacy v1 backport tabs (copy-as-is) | High | unit (per `cherryPickStatus` branch), visual parity | High |
+| Final Product Details states (in-progress/`-`/failure) | High | unit, component | High |
+| Button gating (`areActionsDisabled`, `canRepushBackport`, status==FAILED) | High | unit | High |
+| CI v1 vs v2 rendering branch (`ciVersion`) | High | unit | High |
+| Backports v2 tables + deleted-definition banner placement | Med | unit, component | Med |
+| Merge actions (send-for-review / reopen / fix-issues / cherry-pick-done / repush) | Med | unit (service called with correct payload) | Med |
+| Final product = same component as Tag stage | Med | unit (input wiring) | Med |
+| Route + execution fetch | Low | unit (mapper), smoke | Low |
+
+## Contract / Integration Boundaries
+- **All consumed data-access is migrated into the domains data-access libs** (`domains/business-process/data-access`,
+  `domains/artifact/data-access`) **with unit tests AND contract (pact) tests** — copied from the legacy equivalents and
+  enhanced for the new model shapes. The new code never depends on legacy.
+- **Pact convention:** domains data-access libs have no pact tests today. Add new specs to the **shared
+  `web/libs/contract-tests/src/lib/`** lib (pattern: `scm-service.spec.pact.ts`), importing the new domains services via
+  their barrels. Providers:
+  - `business-process-execution-service` — CI execution payload (`ciVersion`, `backportRequested`,
+    `willPublishFinalProduct`, final product ids), backport executions, BP definitions, and the user-input POST endpoints.
+  - `artifact-management-service` — final product fetch.
+- Reuse/enhance matchers from the legacy CI pact
+  `web/apps/ci-process-mfe/src/contracts/business-process-execution-service.spec.pact.ts`. Verify with **local-pact-verify**.
+
+## Manual / Exploratory Needs
+- Side-by-side comparison of legacy MFE vs new UI for: a v1 process with failed cherry-pick (manual flow), a v1 process with successful backport, a v2 process with mixed backport statuses + a deleted definition, and a process whose final product creation failed.
+- Reference executions provided in the wiki (old BP execution with old backports; the failed/succeeded/deleted-definition backports execution link).
+
+## Test Data & Environment Notes
+- Need a **v1 (legacy)** CI execution and a **v2** CI execution with backports to exercise both paths.
+- Need an execution where `willPublishFinalProduct=true` with a final product in *in-progress* and in *failed* states.
+- Reuse existing CI process mocks/fixtures from `ci-process-mfe` specs where possible.

for old processes.</td></tr><tr class=\"\"><td><div class=\"content-wrapper\"><p>backport tab shows in code shows differnet views for cherry-pick in progress, cherry-pick failed, and cherry-pick successful that are not found in the desing</p><p><ac:image ac:height=\"250\"><ri:attachment ri:filename=\"image-2026-6-4_11-49-56.png\" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:user can keep the design as is and copy code  for backward compatibility</p></td></tr><tr class=\"\"><td><div class=\"content-wrapper\"><p>no need to show branch details for the backport tab?? is it the same one that we have in the branch details? <ac:link><ri:user ri:userkey=\"2c9590c44b400739014b520e2ac40005\" /></ac:link>  <ac:link><ri:user ri:userkey=\"8a9d61079034c95301909bacda370019\" /></ac:link> </p><p><br /></p></div></td><td>part of the legacy tab. to be kept in that tab</td></tr><tr class=\"\"><td><div class=\"content-wrapper\"><p>code shows in backport tab final product publishing component what should we show in the new desing<br /><ac:link><ri:user ri:userkey=\"2c9590c44b400739014b520e2ac40005\" /></ac:link>  <ac:link><ri:user ri:userkey=\"8a9d61079034c95301909bacda370019\" /></ac:link> </p></div></td><td>part of the legacy tab. to be kept in that tab</td></tr><tr class=\"\"><td><div class=\"content-wrapper\"><p>what should we show when backport is in progress? <ac:link><ri:user ri:userkey=\"2c9590c44b400739014b520e2ac40005\" /></ac:link>  <ac:link><ri:user ri:userkey=\"8a9d61079034c95301909bacda370019\" /></ac:link> </p></div></td><td>part of the legacy tab. to be kept in that tab</td></tr><tr class=\"\"><td><div class=\"content-wrapper\"><p>we now fivide failed and passed backports i see we merged in a new desing. but we also show alert for missing definitions what is that and should it be handled differently?? <ac:link><ri:user ri:userkey=\"8a9d608a7209bf8c017239632d210029\" /></ac:link> class=\"content-wrapper\"><p>the on demand backports table shows the processes that we were able to start with their status</p><p><br /></p><p>we need another table</p><p>&quot;Failed to Launch Backports&quot; that shows the BP Definition Name </p><p><br /></p><p>In case a the failed to launch definiton was deleted, we show an erorr banner like it is now with the id of the definition</p><p><br /></p><p>but place it above the tables</p><p><ac:image ac:height=\"250\"><ri:attachment ri:filename=\"image-2026-6-4_16-19-39.png\" +}

index 00000000000..b8b33ad03e5
--- /dev/null
+++ dst://memories/repo/ci-merge-step-migration.md
@@ -0,0 +1,94 @@
+# CI / Build & Test Merge Step Migration (VAL-26642)
+
+Knowledge for migrating the legacy CI **Merge Step / Integrate Changes** (and similar CI stages) from
+the MFE to the new **domains** architecture. Reuse this instead of re-investigating.
+
+## Sources of truth
+- Jira epic VAL-26642: "Same as the upgrade. Add the Final product generation component."
+- Wiki MVF/950870862 (functional Q&A + 3 screenshots). Confluence attachments are downloadable with the
+  CONFLUENCE_TOKEN via `{base}/download/attachments/{pageId}/{filename}` and viewable as images.
+- Refinement artifacts: `devo/.feature/VAL-26642/` (feature-spec, design, story-map, test-strategy, decision-log,
+  plus `stories/story-A-merge-and-final-product.md` and `stories/story-B-backports.md`).
+
+## Target architecture = upgrade-process (NOT validation-process)
+- **Target lib:** `web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/build-and-test-process` (NEW). Legacy v1 backport code goes under a `legacy/` subfolder, **de-remoted**.
+- **Use:** `web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/upgrade-process/integrate-changes-stage` as the template.
+- Pattern: standalone components, signal `input()`/`input.required()`, `rxResource` for fetch, `computed()`/`signal()`,
+  `takeUntilDestroyed`, **no NgRx**, lazy `loadComponent` route in `web/apps/shell/.../business-process-routing.module.ts`.
+- Barrels: `@mxevolve/domains/business-process/{feature,composite-widget,ui,data-access,util}`, `@mxevolve/domains/scm/widget`.
+- **validation-process** (`web/libs/features/business-process/.../validation-process`) is the OLDER MFE+NgRx+`@mxflow/ui/horizontal-timeline` generation — do NOT use it as the template.
+
+## Reusable shared components (already migrated)
+- `mxevolve-stage-container`, `mxevolve-business-process-content-container` (header/footer slots) — `domains/business-process/ui`.
+- `mxevolve-merge-request-stepper` — `domains/scm/widget`; inputs `mergeRequestId`, `projectId`; 3 sub-steps: Under Review / Under Validation / Merge.
+- `mxevolve-retry-merge-request` (Merge button), `mxevolve-fix-issues` (Fix button), `mxevolve-branch-details`,
+  `mxevolve-merge-request-details-form`, `mxevolve-merge-configuration-dropdown`, `mxevolve-autocomplete-reviewers` — `domains/business-process/composite-widget` + `scm/widget`.
+- **Final Product Details: NO domains component exists.** The only one is `mxevolve-final-product-details-component`
+  in `web/libs/features/artifact-manager` (inputs `finalProductId`, `projectId`; output `errorEventEmitter`) but that lib is
+  tagged **`type:legacy`** — a domains lib must NOT depend on it. The migrated upgrade-process **Tag stage does NOT render
+  final product** (only tag name + commit). → **Build a new FP widget in `domains/artifact/widget`** (`type:widget`, `scope:artifact`)
+  + a new **final-product service/models in `domains/artifact/data-access`** (today it has factory-product only). Consume it
+  cross-domain from `business-process/feature` (same pattern as `→ scm/widget`). Reusable by the future Tag-stage migration.
+
+## Legacy source (parity reference)
+- `web/apps/ci-process-mfe/src/app/ci-process/ci-process-execution/ci-process-execution-stages/integrate-changes`
+- 10 components: integrate-changes-stage, integrate-changes (section) + decision, backport-changes,
+  backport-cherry-pick-and-merge-request, backport-manual-cherry-pick, backport-merge-request-view,
+  backport-actions (repush), backport-executions-summary. Plus common: branch-details, send-for-review, merge-request-reopen.
+
+## Behaviour to preserve EXACTLY
+- **No feature flags / no authorization in the merge step.** Flags exist only in a different CI stage
+  (`build-and-test/build-environment-details`). Only route guard is `executionExistsGuard`; shell handles authz.
+  Button gating is purely **state-based**: `areActionsDisabled`, `canRepushBackport`, `ciProcessStatus === FAILED`.
+- **CI version branch** via execution field `ciVersion` (1 vs 2), plus `backportRequested`, `willPublishFinalProduct`,
+  `finalProductId` / `backportFinalProductId`, `integrateDestinationBranch`.
+  - **v1 (legacy):** per-backport tabs. Cherry-pick `@switch (cherryPickStatus)`:
+    `AUTOMATIC_CHERRY_PICK_IN_PROGRESS` → info alert; `AUTOMATIC_CHERRY_PICK_FAILED` → manual-cherry-pick;
+    `COMMITS_CHERRY_PICKED` → backport MR view. Plus repush backport action + per-backport final product.
+    **Copy as-is** (wiki: keep design, copy code for backward compat).
+  - **v2:** single "Backports" tab → keep the **two existing tables** — *On-Demand Backport Executions* (Name+Status) and
+    *Failed Backport Definitions* / failed-to-launch (Definition Name) — rendered with **ag-grid** (shared wrapper); move the
+    deleted/missing-definition error banner **ABOVE** the tables. Figma node 9769-57211 shows the executions table.
+- Info alert wording: v1 "Backport will start after integrating into x passes";
+  v2 "Backport processes will start after changes are integrated into ${integrateDestinationBranch}".
+- Final Product Details: shown when `willPublishFinalProduct` + `finalProductId`; during creation show available
+  info with `-` for missing fields + status In Progress; on failure show "Failed to request publishing a final product".
+- Buttons + endpoints (unchanged): Create New MR → `send-changes-for-review`; Reopen → `reopen-merge-request`;
+  Fix Issues → `fix-integration-issues`; Cherry-Pick Done → `commits-cherry-picked`; Repush → `repush-backport-merge-job`.
+  All under `POST .../business-process/executions/ci-process/{id}/user-input/...`.
+
+## Gotchas
+- Legacy backport/merge-request views are loaded **remotely** from scm-mfe via `RemoteComponentInjectorService`.
+  Remote MFE loading is being REMOVED → copy the legacy view as-is into `legacy/`, **de-remoted** (no injector).
+  For the main merge view use `mxevolve-merge-request-stepper`.
+- Do NOT depend on `features/artifact-manager` (tag `type:legacy`) from `domains/business-process` — build a new domains FP component.
+- Figma: `query_figma.py` needs `requests`; run via `uv run --with requests python query_figma.py ...` from the skill's scripts dir.
+  Export frames as PNG with `export <fileKey> --ids <id> --format png --scale 2 --download <dir>`.
+- `jira_client`/`confluence` scripts run from a different cwd: redirect stdout (`> file`) instead of `--output`.
+- Confluence images: download via `{base}/download/attachments/{pageId}/{filename}` with CONFLUENCE_TOKEN, then view as image.
+
+## Data-access MUST be migrated to domains (no legacy reliance) + contract tests
+- Directive: every service + model the merge step consumes must be **migrated into the new domains data-access libs**
+  (`domains/business-process/data-access`, `domains/artifact/data-access`) **with unit tests AND contract (pact) tests**.
+  Copy from the legacy CI MFE / `features/*` equivalents, then **enhance** (signal/`inject()` style, new model shapes,
+  new barrels). **Never import or depend on legacy.** Data-access is embedded in the stories that need it, NOT a separate story.
+- Services to migrate:
+  - `domains/business-process/data-access`: CI execution fetcher (+ all merge-step fields), CI backport executions,
+    BP definitions (failed-to-launch + deleted-definition). User-input action services (send-for-review, reopen,
+    fix-issues, commits-cherry-picked, repush-backport) **already exist** there (Upgrade uses them) — reuse; add
+    CI-specific payloads only if bodies differ.
+  - `domains/artifact/data-access`: NEW `FinalProductService` + models (today only `factory-product` exists).
+- **Contract-test convention:** domains data-access libs have **NO** pact tests today. Two patterns exist in the repo:
+  per-app `web/apps/*/src/contracts/*.spec.pact.ts` and the shared **`web/libs/contract-tests/src/lib/*.spec.pact.ts`**.
+  Since CI moves to domains *libs* (not an app), put new pact specs in the **shared `web/libs/contract-tests`** lib
+  (pattern: `scm-service.spec.pact.ts` — imports the service via its barrel, tests against a provider). Providers:
+  `business-process-execution-service` (execution + actions + backports), `artifact-management-service` (final product).
+  Reuse/enhance matchers from legacy CI pact `web/apps/ci-process-mfe/src/contracts/business-process-execution-service.spec.pact.ts`.
+  Verify with the **local-pact-verify** skill.
+
+## Suggested slicing
+S1 scaffold lib+stage+route+fetch → then parallel: S2 merge tab, S3 final product, S4 backports v2, S5 backports v1 (copy-as-is), S6 decision/alerts.
+
+**Grouped into 2 execution stories** (each migrates its own data-access + tests + contract tests):
+- **Story A** = S1+S2+S3+S6 (scaffold + merge tab + Final Product Details + decision/alerts).
+- **Story B** = S4+S5 (backports v2 ag-grid + v1 legacy de-remoted). **Depends on Story A** (needs the execution view + stage shell).
diff --git src://memories/repo/ci-process-merge-step-migration.md dst://memories/repo/ci-process-merge-step-migration.md
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..a883382c375
--- /dev/null
+++ dst://memories/repo/ci-process-merge-step-migration.md
@@ -0,0 +1,151 @@
+# CI Process (Build & Test) Migration — Knowledge File
+> Scope: mxflow repo | Created: 2026-06-04 | Feature: VAL-26642
+
+## What This Covers
+How the CI process (Build & Test) migration to domain-libraries works, with focus on the **Merge Step**.
+Use this to avoid re-researching the same stuff in future CI process stories.
+
+---
+
+## Architecture Pattern: New vs Old
+
+| | Old (MFE) | New (Domain Libs) |
+|---|-----------|-------------------|
+| Location | `web/apps/ci-process-mfe/` | `web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/ci-process/` |
+| Reference | `…/ci-process-execution/ci-process-execution-stages/integrate-changes/` | Same folder pattern as upgrade-process |
+| State management | NgRx store (`getCiProcessExecution` selector) | signals + `rxResource` |
+| Components | NgModule-based | Standalone |
+| Services | Local to MFE | `web/libs/domains/business-process/data-access/src/lib/ci-process/` |
+
+---
+
+## CI Process API Endpoints
+Base: `{gatewayUrl}projects/{projectId}/business-process/executions/ci-process`
+
+| Action | Method | Path |
+|--------|--------|------|
+| Send for review (trigger merge) | POST | `/{processId}/user-input/send-changes-for-review` |
+| Fix integration issues | POST | `/{processId}/user-input/fix-integration-issues` |
+| Reopen merge request | POST | `/{processId}/user-input/reopen-merge-request` |
+| Mark cherry-pick done | POST | `/{processId}/user-input/commits-cherry-picked` |
+| Repush backport MR | POST | `/{processId}/user-input/repush-backport-merge-job` |
+| Proceed with predefined inputs | POST | `/{processId}/user-input/proceed-with-predefined-inputs` |
+
+**Important**: These differ from Upgrade Process paths (`/binary-upgrade`). Always use CI-specific services.
+
+---
+
+## CI-Specific Features vs Upgrade Process
+
+| Feature | CI Process | Upgrade Process |
+|---------|-----------|-----------------|
+| Backports tab | YES — CI v1: individual tabs per backport; CI v2: single summary tab | NO |
+| Final Product Details in merge sub-step | YES — shown after merge completes | NO |
+| ciVersion gate | YES — `ciVersion: 1 | 2` controls backport tab display | N/A |
+| Action: Reopen MR | YES | NO |
+| Action: Cherry-Pick Done | YES (backport specific) | NO |
+| Action: Repush Backport | YES (backport specific) | NO |
+| `sendChangesForReview` payload | Has extra backport fields: `backportChanges`, `backportMergeConfigurationIds`, `backportInputs` | Simpler payload |
+
+---
+
+## Shared Components (reuse as-is, no modification)
+- `MergeRequestStepperComponent` — `web/libs/domains/scm/widget/src/lib/merge-request-stepper/`
+  - Inputs: `mergeRequestId`, `projectId`, `statuses?` (override step statuses)
+  - Fetches MR via `getMergeRequestById(projectId, mergeRequestId)`
+  - Extended for VAL-26642: new optional `finalProductPublishing` input
+- `ReviewStageDetailsComponent` — `web/libs/domains/scm/widget/src/lib/review-stage-details/`
+  - Has authorization guard: `update_priority` on `scm/merge_request` for priority selector
+- `UnderValidationStageDetailsComponent` — `web/libs/domains/scm/widget/src/lib/under-validation-stage-details/`
+  - Shows: queue position, merge mode, rebase status, commit diffs, scenario runs
+  - Contains `ScenarioRunsComponent` (`test/widget`) and `PaginatedCommitsDifferenceComponent` (`scm/widget`)
+- `MergeStageDetailsComponent` — `web/libs/domains/scm/widget/src/lib/merge-stage-details/`
+  - Shows: merge header verb, merge status tag, failure reason
+  - Extended for VAL-26642: optional `finalProductPublishing` in `MergeStageData`
+- `FinalProductDetailsComponent` — `web/libs/features/artifact-manager/src/lib/final-product/final-product-component/`
+  - Shows: ID, Branch, Commit, Validation Level (labeled "Tag" in Figma), Status, Factory Product, Software Product
+- `MergeRequestDetailsFormComponent` — `web/libs/domains/business-process/composite-widget/src/lib/upgrade-process/merge-request-details-form/`
+  - Reusable form for Send Merge dialog
+
+---
+
+## Upgrade Process as Migration Template
+
+The Upgrade Process is the canonical reference for all new BP migrations.
+Path: `web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/upgrade-process/integrate-changes-stage/`
+
+| Upgrade Process Component | CI Process Equivalent | Status |
+|--------------------------|----------------------|--------|
+| `IntegrateChangesStageComponent` | `CiIntegrateChangesStageComponent` | NEW in VAL-26642 |
+| `RetryMergeRequestComponent` (composite-widget) | `RetryMergeRequestCiComponent` | NEW in VAL-26642 |
+| `FixIssuesComponent` (composite-widget) | `FixIssuesCiComponent` | NEW in VAL-26642 |
+| `SendChangesForReviewService` (data-access) | `CiProcessExecutionService` | NEW in VAL-26642 |
+| `UpgradeProcessStateUpdaterService` | `CiProcessStateUpdaterService` | NEW in VAL-26642 |
+
+---
+
+## CI v1 vs CI v2 Backport Architecture
+
+**CI v1** (legacy): `ciVersion === 1`
+- Each backport destination branch gets its own tab: "Backport to {branch}"
+- Each tab shows `BackportChangesComponent` (cherry-pick + MR + repush)
+- Cherry-pick state machine per backport: `AUTOMATIC_IN_PROGRESS` → `AUTOMATIC_FAILED` → `COMMITS_CHERRY_PICKED`
+
+**CI v2** (current default): `ciVersion === 2`
+- Single "Backports" tab shows `CiBackportsSummaryComponent`
+- Summary table: Name + Status (links to backport execution details)
+- No per-backport cherry-pick UI
+
+**Rule**: Always implement both; gate with `@if (ciVersion() === 1)` / `@if (ciVersion() === 2)`.
+`ciVersion` comes from the execution model (check `BuildAndTestProcessExecution.integrateChangesStage`).
+
+---
+
+## Final Product Details — Figma Fields Mapping
+| Figma Label | Component Property | Notes |
+|-------------|-------------------|-------|
+| Final Product ID | `finalProduct.id` | |
+| Commit | `finalProduct.configurationCommitId` | |
+| Status | `finalProduct.status` (tagged) | |
+| Branch | `finalProduct.branch` | |
+| Tag | `finalProduct.validationLevel` | Figma says "Tag", code says "Validation Level" — same field, verify with designer |
+| Failure Reason | `finalProduct.failureMessage` | |
+| Factory Product — ID | `finalProduct.factoryProduct.id` | |
+| Factory Product — Type | `finalProduct.factoryProduct.type` | e.g. "CANDIDATE" |
+| Software Product — ID | `finalProduct.softwareProduct.id` | |
+| Software Product — Version | `finalProduct.softwareProduct.version` | |
+| Software Product — Change List | `finalProduct.softwareProduct.revision` | |
+
+---
+
+## File Discovery Quick Reference
+```
+# Old MFE Merge Step (source for migration):
+web/apps/ci-process-mfe/src/app/ci-process/ci-process-execution/ci-process-execution-stages/integrate-changes/
+
+# New arch merge step (this feature):
+web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/ci-process/integrate-changes-stage/
+
+# Upgrade process reference (template to follow):
+web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/upgrade-process/integrate-changes-stage/
+
+# Shared stepper (scm/widget):
+web/libs/domains/scm/widget/src/lib/merge-request-stepper/
+web/libs/domains/scm/widget/src/lib/review-stage-details/
+web/libs/domains/scm/widget/src/lib/under-validation-stage-details/
+web/libs/domains/scm/widget/src/lib/merge-stage-details/
+
+# CI data-access (new):
+web/libs/domains/business-process/data-access/src/lib/ci-process/
+
+# CI composite widgets (new):
+web/libs/domains/business-process/composite-widget/src/lib/ci-process/
+```
+
+---
+
+## Open Items (as of VAL-26642 planning)
+- `ciVersion` field name on `BuildAndTestProcessExecution` execution model — verify before S2
+- CI v1 Send Merge dialog backport config fields — check wiki or ask BE
+- "Tag" vs "Validation Level" label discrepancy — confirm with designer
+- Wiki page images not readable (set CONFLUENCE_TOKEN in devo/.env)
diff --git src://memories/repo/legacy-to-domain-lib-migration.md dst://memories/repo/legacy-to-domain-lib-migration.md
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..7335bf70eef
--- /dev/null
+++ dst://memories/repo/legacy-to-domain-lib-migration.md
@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+# Legacy MFE UI → business-process Domain-Library Migration Playbook
+
+Reusable guide for migrating a legacy run page (header + stepper + tabs + data-access) from the old
+MFE/`features` libs into the new `web/libs/domains/business-process/*` domain-library architecture.
+Reference implementation: **Upgrade Process**. First reuse: **Build & Test / CI Process** (VAL-26634).
+
+## Layer map (where each piece goes)
+| Concern | New library | Path pattern |
+|---------|-------------|--------------|
+| Page container + stepper + stages | `feature` | `web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/<process>` |
+| Run header, tabs, abort, branch-details | `composite-widget` | `.../composite-widget/src/lib/<process>/execution-run-header` (+ shared `branch-details`, `execution-abort-button`) |
+| Run/Activity details panel | `widget` | `.../widget/src/lib/<process>/activity-run-details` |
+| Status tag, expiry chip, containers, alerts | `ui` | `.../ui/src/lib/{execution-status-tag,expiry-chip,stage-container,business-process-content-container,execution-alert-display}` |
+| DTOs + enums | `util` | `.../util/src/lib/{<process>-execution.ts,stage.ts,execution-status.ts,execution-family.ts}` |
+| HTTP services + models + specs | `data-access` | `.../data-access/src/lib/<process>/{...}.service.ts` (+ `models/`) |
+
+## Reuse-first checklist (don't rebuild these)
+- `execution-status-tag` (status badge) — `@mxevolve/domains/business-process/ui`
+- `expiry-chip` / `mxevolve-expiry-chip` (expiry warning; show unless finished) — `ui`
+- `execution-abort-button` — `composite-widget`
+- `branch-details` (development + commits + latest merge request via `MergeRequestService` + `rxResource`) — `composite-widget`
+- `StepperComponent` — `@mxevolve/shared/ui/primitive`
+- `MxevolveIllustrationComponent` — `@mxevolve/shared/ui/primitive` (empty/loading illustrations, e.g. create-branch). Needs `ILLUSTRATIONS_PATH` provided in app config.
+- `stage-container`, `business-process-content-container`, `execution-alert-display` — `ui`
+
+## State & data patterns
+- **No NgRx.** Use **rxResource + signals**. Wrap fetches: `rxResource({ params: () => (...), stream: ({params}) => service.fetch(...) })`.
+- Derive UI state with `computed()`; sync URL `?step=` with stepper selection via `effect()`.
+- A `<process>-state-updater.service` triggers `router.navigateByUrl(...).then(() => window.location.reload())` after state-changing actions (mirrors upgrade-process).
+- HTTP services inject `APP_CONFIG.gatewayUrl`; return typed observables.
+
+## Stepper conventions
+- `steps = computed(() => [{ id, title, status, tooltip? }])` — `StepDefinition` is in `web/libs/shared/ui/primitive/src/lib/stepper/step.ts`.
+- `StepStatus = "active" | "inactive" | "completed" | "failed"` (NO `skipped`/`pending` value).
+- Status mapping (upgrade): `PASSED→completed`, `RUNNING|PENDING_INPUT→active`, `FAILED→failed`, `NOT_STARTED|STOPPED|NA→inactive`. **`SKIPPED`**: if the skipped step should NOT be clickable, map to `inactive`. If the skipped step should be clickable (user can inspect), map to `completed`. **Check with Khaled per process.** Note: `inactive` renders `circle_dot_inactive` (grey dot); a grey check requires adding a `skipped` non-clickable `StepStatus` to the shared stepper.
+- **Start/End dates**: put them in `tooltip` per step — the shared `StepperComponent` already renders `step.tooltip` via `pTooltip`. The `Stage` model (`util/src/lib/stage.ts`) already has `startDate?`/`endDate?` from the API; no extra fetching.
+- Skip visual: if you need a skipped step to show grey **+ check**, the shared stepper has no `skipped` status — either append " - Skipped" to the title and map `SKIPPED→completed`, or extend the shared `StepStatus`.
+- Default step selection: pick active step; else last completed/failed; allow `?step=` override via effect.
+- Tooltips everywhere use PrimeNG `pTooltip` (`import { Tooltip } from "primeng/tooltip"`), with `tooltipPosition` + `tooltipDisabled`.
+
+## Routing conventions
+- Route lives in `web/apps/shell/src/app/business-process/business-process-routing.module.ts` as `<path>/execution/:executionId` (+ a sibling `executions` listing).
+- Route `data` carries `action/package/resource` and `attributes.familyId`; `resolve.projectId = ProjectIdGuardInputResolver`; guarded by `AuthorizationGuard` + `executionExistsGuard`.
+- Define the path as a constant in `web/libs/shared/core/config/src/lib/microfrontend-paths.ts` (e.g. `BINARY_UPGRADE_MFE_PATH = "upgrade-processes"`) and use it in the `<process>-state-updater.service` `navigateByUrl`.
+- The execution-view reads `projectId`/`executionId` as `input.required<string>()` (route params) and `?step=` from `ActivatedRoute`, syncing back to the URL via an effect.
+
+## Run header structure (clone from upgrade-process `execution-run-header`)
+- Card with: run name, `execution-status-tag`, `mxevolve-expiry-chip`, tab nav, tab content `@switch`, `execution-abort-button`.
+- Tabs vary per process: upgrade = Run Details + Branch Details + Reference Environment; CI = Run Details + Branch Details (NO Reference Environment).
+- Activity/Run Details field set differs per process — verify against the real API response, not the template.
+
+## Testing conventions
+- Jest per library (`jest.config.ts`, `src/test-setup.ts`).
+- Components: `@testing-library/angular` `render()` + `ng-mocks` `MockComponent(...)`.
+- HTTP services: `provideHttpClient()` + `provideHttpClientTesting()` + `HttpTestingController` (assert method/URL/body, then `verify()`); provide `APP_CONFIG` with a test `gatewayUrl`.
+- Use a `build<Process>Execution(overrides)` test-data factory.
+- Run web tests via the **web-unit-test-runner** skill (Jest 30 + Nx can hang otherwise).
+
+## Wire-in & legacy removal
+- Mount the new `feature` page either via a shell route (`web/apps/shell/src/app/business-process/business-process-routing.module.ts`) OR inside the existing MFE (e.g. `ci-process-mfe`), depending on the decommission strategy.
+- Before deleting legacy components, run **find-references** to catch sibling-screen usages; remove dead imports.
+- Legacy locations to look for: `web/libs/features/business-process/src/lib/...` and `web/apps/<x>-mfe/...`.
+
+## Gotchas learned
+- `execution-family.ts` may already contain the family enum (e.g. `USER_STORY_BUILD_AND_TEST`) — reuse it.
+- Legacy stepper used `StatusBarComponent` from `@mxflow/ui/horizontal-timeline` (different from the new `StepperComponent`) — don't port the old stepper; re-model on the shared one.
+- Reading Jira attachment images: download with `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $JIRA_TOKEN" <secure/attachment/...>` to a local file, then view it.
+- Confluence page body is storage-format HTML; strip tags to read Q&A tables (`devo-run.sh confluence fetch-page --page-id <id>`).
+- No backend/contract changes for these UI migrations — endpoints are reused; the real risk is DTO field-name drift vs the legacy `*ApiModel` mapper.
diff --git src://memories/repo/val-26641-build-test-migration.md dst://memories/repo/val-26641-build-test-migration.md
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..aedd4eaee59
--- /dev/null
+++ dst://memories/repo/val-26641-build-test-migration.md
@@ -0,0 +1,102 @@
+# VAL-26641 — CI Build & Test step migration (Feature Architect research)
+
+Feature = migrate the **Build & Test** stage BODY of the CI process from legacy MFE to new
+domains architecture, per new Figma. Last of 4 features (VAL-26634 shell, 26640 prepare-setup,
+26641 build&test, 26642 merge). Branch `ui/VAL-26641`. Artifacts → `.github/devo/.feature/VAL-26641/`.
+
+## Legacy source (must preserve EVERYTHING)
+`web/apps/ci-process-mfe/src/app/ci-process/ci-process-execution/ci-process-execution-stages/build-and-test/`
+- `build-and-test-stage.component.{ts,html}` — NgRx-driven (`getCiProcessExecution` selector).
+- Legacy html section ORDER: card → "Build & Test" header → if errorMessage: error-alert →
+  else if !readyForBuildAndTest: info-alert "step is loading, refresh" (Figma → create-branch
+  illustration) → else: (1) cherry-pick alert, (2) p-panel "Branch Details" → branch-details
+  (commits table), (3) IF showEnvironmentDetails: p-panel "Build Environment Details" →
+  build-environment-section, (4) IF showTechnicalReseedDetails: technical-reseed-operation,
+  (5) test-section (p-panel "Tests": Test Selection + Execution Results), (6) build-and-test-actions
+  (Create MR button popup).
+- Subfolders: build-and-test-action/, build-and-test-cherry-pick/, build-and-test-result/,
+  build-environment-details/ (environment/ + scenario/), model/, test-section/
+  (build-and-test-run-scenario/ + build-and-test-scenario-executions/).
+- `build-environment-details/environment/build-environment-details.component.ts`: gates
+  **Open Config Editor** via feature flag `workspace-configuration-editor-ui` (FeatureFlagResolver).
+- Model: `web/libs/features/business-process/src/lib/build-and-test/` — `BuildAndTestProcessExecution`,
+  stage `build-and-test-process-build-and-test-stage.ts` fields: scenarioExecutionGroup,
+  technicalReseedExecutionGroupId, readyForBuildAndTest, cherryPickRunning, cherryPickFailed,
+  status (BUILD_AND_TEST_STAGE_ID="BUILD_AND_TEST"). integrateChangesStage.latestMergeJobId.
+
+## Visibility / authorizations / flags to preserve (CRITICAL)
+- `showEnvironmentDetails` = !input.buildEnvironment.skipEnvironmentDeployment.
+- `showTechnicalReseedDetails` = buildAndTestStage.technicalReseedExecutionGroupId != null.
+- `isUserInterventionDisabled` = stage status not PENDING_INPUT/RUNNING → disables buttons.
+- Feature flag `workspace-configuration-editor-ui` → Open Config Editor. Hidden in automerge (wiki).
+- Feature flag `config-audit` → Config Audit (project-mfe uses it today; NOT yet in build&test).
+- `can-repush` permission (mergeDevelopmentState.canRepush) → Repush Backport.
+- `ScenarioExecutionGroupPermissionWarningMessage` → warning map when running TPK.
+
+## NEW additions in this migration (in code/wiki but NOT in legacy build&test UI)
+1. **Config Audit ▾** split-button. ALREADY EXISTS as
+   `web/libs/features/environment/src/lib/environment-config-audit/button/environment-config-audit-button.component.ts`
+   (selector `mxevolve-environment-config-audit-button`, inputs projectId+environmentId).
+   Color-codes button (success/warn/danger) by `configurationLintingResult.resultStatus`
+   (PASS/WARNING/FAIL), dropdown lists `artifacts[]` (CSV/HTML) via ConfigAuditArtifactMenuBuilder.
+   Models: `environment-config-audit/models/systematic-config-audit.models.ts`
+   (SystematicConfigAuditOperationsResponse, RequestStatus, RequestResultType,
+   ConfigurationLintingOperationResult, ConfigurationLintingMode FULL/DELTA). Service endpoint
+   `GET /projects/{projectId}/environments/{environmentId}/systematic-config-audit`. → must be
+   migrated/wrapped into domains and added to env actions bar.
+2. **Repush latest TPK** + **TPK Details** buttons (wiki note 4) — NEW, placed OUTSIDE existing
+   env-actions component. (Repush Scenario exists in scenario-runs; "Scenario Details" does NOT.)
+3. Loading state → **create-branch illustration** (VAL-26634 provides illustration) instead of info-alert.
+4. Story IDs (jira-user-stories chips) must move OFF NgRx store → take as input or refetch (wiki note 5).
+5. Commits table = SAME as Branch Details tab (wiki note 6) — only the commits table duplicated.
+
+## New architecture target + reuse map (VERIFIED)
+- Mirror migrated **upgrade-process**: `web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/upgrade-process/`
+  (stages convert-binary-stage/, integrate-changes-stage/, etc.; view uses rxResource + stepper +
+  selectedStepId synced to URL `step` query param). Build&test feature lib DOES NOT EXIST yet
+  (must create `build-and-test-process/` next to upgrade-process/). Target model lib =
+  `build-and-test-process` per VAL-26642.
+- **mxevolve-environment-status-panel** (`domains/environment/widget/.../environment-status-panel/`)
+  = composite env-actions bar (status tag + Services/Open MX.3/Connect DB/Connect Applicative/Copy/
+  Details). inputs projectId+environmentId (required, signals), output environmentPanelError.
+  Exported from `domains/environment/widget/src/index.ts`. **Does NOT include Open Config Editor
+  or Config Audit** — must add both here.
+- **mxevolve-scenario-runs** (`domains/test/widget/src/lib/scenario-runs/`) = full TPK panel; RENDERS
+  environment-status-panel INTERNALLY when showEnvironmentDetails && env id. So Build env bar + Test
+  env bar come FREE from scenario-runs. Inputs incl projectId(req), subContextId, scenarioRunIds,
+  showEnvironmentDetails, showHistory, showBulkRerun, detailsExpandedByDefault, warningMessageMap,
+  filter, topBarTemplate (#topBar ContentChild). Output scenarioChanged. (VAL-26640 wraps it with
+  subContextId="PREPARE_BUILD_ENVIRONMENT".)
+- Open Config Editor exists only in features:
+  `features/environment/.../environment-actions/environment-workspace-configuration-editor-button/`
+  (label "Open Config Editor", opens `/app/{projectId}/environments/{environmentId}/configuration-editor`).
+  Must port to domains + signals, add to environment-status-panel.
+- **Technical Reseed**: `features/environment/src/lib/technical-reseed/technical-reseed.component.ts`
+  (selector `mxevolve-technical-reseed-operation`, inputs executionGroupId+projectId+infraGroup+
+  targetBranch req, output reseedLaunchedSuccessfully). Uses NgRx ExecutionGroupsStoreModule +
+  legacy artifact-manager FinalProductService. Launch modal fields: Final Product Tag, Env
+  Definition, Maintenance Level → POST `projects/{projectId}/technical-reseed-execution-groups/{egId}/launch-reseed`
+  (LaunchTechnicalReseedOperationRequest). Operation rows have dumpIds[] (see-more). Status enum
+  PENDING/RUNNING/PASSED/FAILED/ABORTED. Pact `web/pacts/web-mxenv-management.json`. → needs domains
+  port (drop NgRx, use rxResource; replace artifact-manager FinalProductService with domains artifact widget).
+- Commits table reuse: `domains/scm/widget/.../merge-request-commits/` (ag-grid) via branch-details
+  (`business-process/composite-widget/.../branch-details/` → DevelopmentDetailsComponent).
+- Merge action (Create MR popup): VAL-26642 OWNS merge; build&test just needs the Create-MR trigger
+  button. send-changes-for-review / reopen-merge-request / repush-backport endpoints under
+  `gateway/projects/{projectId}/business-process/executions/ci-process/{ciProcessId}/user-input/...`.
+- Data-access: copy upgrade `ExecutionFetcherService` pattern; VAL-26634 owns the ci-process GET
+  (`executions/ci-process/:id` → BuildAndTestProcessExecution) + state-updater (page reload).
+- StepStatus "skipped" added by VAL-26634.
+
+## MFE removal (Khaled wants it tracked) — ~8 refs, do LAST
+shell business-process-routing.module.ts CI route; shell/src/decl.d.ts `ci-process-mfe/Module`;
+shell tailwind.config.js content path; app-layout.component.ts CI_PROCESS_ROUTE; environment.ts
+ciProcessMfeUrl; web/tools/local-dev/project.json serve; pact
+business-process-execution-service.spec.pact.ts; web/libs/config/src/lib/mfe-urls.ts CI_PROCESS_MFE_PATH.
+upgrade-process-mfe + validation-process-mfe already deleted (pattern). Shell route can only switch
+once ALL 4 CI stages exist in domains → removal belongs to a final cleanup story.
+
+## Recommended execution order
+VAL-26634 FIRST (foundation: shell + ci data-access + stepper + skipped + run header). Then 26640 /
+26641 / 26642 are independent stage bodies plugged into stepper → can run in parallel (recommend 2
+at a time). MFE removal last (its own story or tail of 26641/26642).

+# VAL-26634 Feature Refinement — Session Notes
+
+## Feature
+[UI/UX][CI] Build & Test Run Page Header & Run Stepper (Epic, ATLANTIS, owner MOUZAYA Myriam).
+Migrate LEGACY Build & Test / CI process run header + stepper → NEW domain-lib architecture,
+modeled on the already-migrated **Upgrade Process**.
+
+## Images (downloaded to .github/devo/.feature/VAL-26634/images/)
+- 1753153.png: Config Parameters = Repository, Configuration Branch, Configuration Parent Branch
+- 1753155.png: Stepper skip state = "Prepare Setup - Skipped" (grey+check) -> Build & Test (active) -> Merge
+
+## Wiki Q&A (page 950870852)
+- Stepper stages need Start Date + End Date (exists in legacy; upgrade missing it by mistake).
+- Expiry tag: always shown unless process finished (same as upgrade).
+- Branch details commits: same logic as upgrade.
+- Abort: use execution-abort-button (done).
+- BP status: use execution-status-tag. Expiry: use mxevolve-expiry-chip.
+- Branch Details same as upgrade. NO Reference Environment (unlike upgrade).
+- Activity Details "a bit different".
+- Stepper: reuse common upgrade stepper.
+- On first CI process creation: replace "business process execution is currently loading..." with create-branch illustration (from validation process). Shared MxevolveIllustrationComponent in @mxevolve/shared/ui/primitive.
+
+## NEW architecture (Upgrade Process template) — layers
+- feature: web/libs/domains/business-process/feature/src/lib/upgrade-process (execution-view = page+stepper)
+- composite-widget: .../composite-widget/src/lib/upgrade-process/execution-run-header (HEADER, tabs, abort),
+  branch-details, execution-abort-button
+- widget: .../widget/src/lib/upgrade-process/activity-run-details (run details panel)
+- ui: .../ui/src/lib/{execution-status-tag, expiry-chip, stage-container, business-process-content-container, execution-alert-display}
+- util: .../util/src/lib/{upgrade-process-execution, stage, execution-status, execution-family, branch-creation-details}
+  - execution-family.ts ALREADY has USER_STORY_BUILD_AND_TEST = "user-story-build-and-test"
+- data-access: .../data-access/src/lib/upgrade-process/{execution-fetcher, listing, abort, ...} + models, all with *.spec.ts (HttpTestingController)
+- State: rxResource + signals (NO NgRx). upgrade-process-state-updater.service.
+- Route: web/apps/shell/src/app/business-process/business-process-routing.module.ts -> upgrade-processes/execution/:executionId
+- Stepper: StepperComponent from @mxevolve/shared/ui/primitive. steps computed() w/ status mapping.
+
+## LEGACY build-and-test
+- Header: web/libs/features/business-process/src/lib/business-process-execution-progress/business-process-execution-progress.component.ts (selector mxevolve-business-process-execution-progress)
+- Details container + stepper: web/apps/ci-process-mfe/src/app/ci-process/ci-process-execution/ci-process-execution-details/...
+- Stepper: StatusBarComponent from @mxflow/ui/horizontal-timeline; Stage has startDate/endDate; SKIPPED status supported (prepare-build-stage)
+- 4 stages: create-branch -> prepare-build -> build-and-test -> integrate-changes
+- Data: web/libs/features/business-process/src/lib/build-and-test/build-and-test-process-execution-fetcher/...
+  - GET {gateway}projects/{projectId}/business-process/executions/ci-process/{ciProcessId}
+  - user-input POSTs: send-changes-for-review, commits-cherry-picked, repush-backport-merge-request, reopen-merge-request, fix-integration-issues, proceed-with-predefined-inputs
+  - Models: BuildAndTestProcessExecutionApiModel (api), BuildAndTestProcessExecution (domain)
+- MFE: web/apps/ci-process-mfe, route /ci-process/execution/:executionId
+- User wants: reuse new header in ci-process-mfe for now; delete MFE later when all steps migrated.
+
+## Image stepper steps (new): Prepare Setup (skippable) -> Build & Test -> Merge (3 visible)
+vs legacy 4 (create-branch may become the "loading/create-branch illustration" pre-state).
+
+## Open decisions to confirm with Khaled (see questions)
+1. New folder/route name: build-and-test-process vs ci-process
+2. Final step list + labels + which steps skippable
+3. Tabs: Run Details + Branch Details only (no Reference Env) - confirm
+4. Run Details field set (General/Config Params/Build Scenario/Infra Params)
+5. Consumption: wire shell route + consume in ci-process-mfe? story granularity
+6. Start/End date per stage: extend shared stepper or show in stage header
+7. Knowledge file location
+
+## Preferences: guided mode (pause each phase), ask commit + branch each time, call user "Khaled".

           if (ciProcessExecution.id === this.ciProcessId) {
             this.ciProcessExecution = ciProcessExecution;
             this.errorMessage = ciProcessExecution.errorMessage;
             this.status = ciProcessExecution.status;
             this.notStarted = this.resolveNotStarted();
             this.executionStages = this.getExecutionStages();
             this.navigateToWantedStage(ciProcessExecution);
             this.title.setTitle(
               `BP Execution - ${this.ciProcessExecution.name} - ${this.projectName}`
             );
+            this.fetchDomainExecution();
             this.loading = false;
           }
         },
         error: (error) => {
           this.store.dispatch(
             CiProcessActions.updateErrorMessage({ message: error })
           );
           this.loading = false;
         },
       });
@@ -117,20 +122,29 @@ export class CiProcessExecutionDetailsComponent implements OnInit, OnDestroy {
       this.genericSelectedStage =
         this.buildAndTestProcessExecutionMapperService.toStage(stageToSelect);
     }
   }
 
   ngOnDestroy(): void {
     this.destroy$.next({});
     this.destroy$.complete();
   }
 
+  private fetchDomainExecution(): void {
+    this.domainExecutionFetcher
+      .fetchExecution(this.projectId, this.ciProcessId)
+      .pipe(takeUntil(this.destroy$))
+      .subscribe((execution) => {
+        this.domainExecution = execution;
+      });
+  }
+
   private getExecutionStages(): Stage[] {
     this.ciProcessStages = [
       this.ciProcessExecution!.createBranchStage,
       this.ciProcessExecution!.prepareBuildStage,
       this.ciProcessExecution!.buildAndTestStage,
       this.ciProcessExecution!.integrateChangesStage,
     ];
     return this.buildAndTestProcessExecutionMapperService.toExecutionStages(
       this.ciProcessStages
     );

       environmentConfigAuditService.retrieveSystematicConfigAudits(
         PROJECT_ID,
         ENVIRONMENT_ID
       )
     );
     expect(result).not.toBeNull();
   });
 
+  it("should verify the domains systematic config audit contract", async () => {
+    await provider.addInteraction({
+      state: "environment systematic config audit can be fetched",
+      uponReceiving:
+        "a domains request to fetch environment systematic config audit",
+      withRequest: {
+        method: "GET",
+        path: `/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/environments/${ENVIRONMENT_ID}/systematic-config-audit`,
+      },
+      willRespondWith: {
+        status: 200,
+        headers: {
+          "Content-Type": "application/json",
+        },
+        body: {
+          environmentId: Matchers.string(),
+          operationId: Matchers.string(),
+          targetCommitId: Matchers.string(),
+          baselineCommitId: Matchers.string(),
+          requestStatus: Matchers.string(),
+          requestStatusMessage: Matchers.string(),
+          requestResultStatus: Matchers.string(),
+          requestResultMessage: Matchers.string(),
+          configurationLintingResult: {
+            resultStatus: Matchers.string(),
+            resultMessage: Matchers.string(),
+            startTime: Matchers.string(),
+            endTime: Matchers.string(),
+            duration: Matchers.string(),
+            mode: Matchers.string(),
+            artifacts: Matchers.eachLike(Matchers.string()),
+          },
+        },
+      },
+    });
+
+    const result = await lastValueFrom(
+      systematicConfigAuditService.retrieveSystematicConfigAudit(
+        PROJECT_ID,
+        ENVIRONMENT_ID
+      )
+    );
+    expect(result).not.toBeNull();
+  });

+<p-card class="bp-header-card">
+  <div class="flex items-center justify-between gap-4 flex-wrap">
+    <div class="flex items-center gap-3">
+      <span class="text-2xl font-bold text-color">
+        {{ execution().name }}
+      </span>
+      <mxevolve-execution-status-tag [status]="execution().status" />
+      @if (execution().expiryDate && !execution().endDate) {
+      <mxevolve-expiry-chip [expiryDate]="execution().expiryDate!" />
+      }
+    </div>
+    <div class="flex items-center gap-3">
+      <div class="flex items-center gap-4">
+        @for (tab of tabOptions(); track tab.value) {
+        <span
+          [class]="
+            selectedTab() === tab.value
+              ? 'px-3 py-2 rounded text-muted-color bg-surface-100 cursor-pointer'
+              : 'px-3 py-2 rounded text-muted-color cursor-pointer'
+          "
+          (click)="selectTab(tab)"
+          (keyup)="selectTab(tab)"
+        >
+          {{ tab.label }}
+          @if (tab.value === 'branch-details' && commitsBehindCount() > 0) {
+          <mxevolve-icon
+            name="warning"
+            size="sm"
+            class="text-yellow-500 ml-2"
+            [pTooltip]="commitsBehindTooltip()"
+            tooltipPosition="top"
+          />
+          }
+        </span>
+        }
+      </div>
+      <mxevolve-execution-abort-button
+        [projectId]="execution().projectId"
+        [processId]="execution().id"
+        [status]="execution().status"
+        [familyId]="familyId"
+        (aborted)="reloadProcess()"
+      />
+    </div>
+  </div>
+
+  @if (selectedTab()) {
+  <p-divider />
+  } @switch (selectedTab()) { @case ('activity-run-details') {
+  <mxevolve-build-and-test-activity-run-details [execution]="execution()" />
+  } @case ('branch-details') {
+  <mxevolve-branch-details
+    [projectId]="execution().projectId"
+    [processId]="execution().id"
+    [branchCreation]="branchCreationDetails()!"
+    [development]="development()!"
+    [commitsBehindCount]="commitsBehindCount()"
+  />
+  } }
+</p-card>

index 00000000000..f950bf98365
